primary_concern: Sepsis with concurrent acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) and low platelets (88-95 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. AKI Stage 3 (creatinine 8.9 mg/dL) is present but the value was excluded as an outlier due to rapid 6.5 mg/dL rise in <6 hours. Persistent thrombocytopenia (<100 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulopathy scoring criteria.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L + WBC >12 K/uL + elevated creatinine meet Sepsis-3 criteria
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine elevation meets KDIGO Stage 1 criteria (though Stage 3 value excluded as outlier)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coag score ≥1
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but no evidence of vasopressor-dependent hypotension
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent MAP ≥65 + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Order urgent infectious workup including blood cultures
Assess for fluid responsiveness and initiate resuscitation
Monitor platelet trends and consider transfusion if <50 K/uL
Recheck creatinine with confirmatory testing due to outlier exclusion
doctor_handoff: Review excluded creatinine outlier for specimen integrity. Confirm presence of hypotension/vasopressor use to rule in septic shock. Evaluate for source control of infection.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2, Hgb 9.8, Bilirubin 1.4
Lactate elevates to 4.2 mmol/L with platelets 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
